schema.description: MCP server exposing Airtable workspace, base, table, and record CRUD operations with optional filtering, pagination, and formula-based queries.

tools.name: list_bases
tools.description: Retrieve all bases accessible to the authenticated Airtable workspace.

bases.description: List of bases in the workspace.

id.description: The unique identifier of the Airtable base.

name.description: The display name of the Airtable base.

permissionLevel.description: Access level granted to the caller within this base.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: Fetch schema and metadata for every table inside a specified base.

base_id.description: The unique identifier of the Airtable base whose tables are to be listed.
The unique identifier of the Airtable base whose tables are returned.

tables.description: Collection of tables defined in the base.

id.description: The unique identifier of the table.

name.description: The display name of the table.

description.description: Optional summary or purpose of the table.

fields.description: List of field schemas (name, type, options) within the table.

views.description: List of views (grid, form, gallery, etc.) configured for the table.

tools.name: list_records
tools.description: Query and paginate through records in a table, optionally filtered by a view or Airtable formula.

base_id.description: The unique identifier of the Airtable base containing the table.

table_id.description: The table ID or table name whose records are to be listed.

view.description: Optional view name or ID that pre-orders or pre-filters the records.

filter_by_formula.description: Optional Airtable formula string used to filter the returned records.

max_records.description: Maximum number of records to return (default 100, max 100).

offset.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous call to fetch the next page.

records.description: Array of record objects returned by the query.

id.description: The unique identifier of the record.

createdTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the record was created.

fields.description: Key-value map of field names or IDs to their respective values for this record.

offset.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page of results; absent when no further pages exist.

tools.name: create_record
tools.description: Insert a new record into the specified table with the provided field values.

base_id.description: The unique identifier of the Airtable base containing the table.

table_id.description: The table ID or table name where the new record will be created.

fields.description: Map of field names or IDs to the values assigned to the new record.

id.description: The unique identifier of the newly created record.

createdTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the record was created.

fields.description: Complete set of field values as stored for the new record.

tools.name: update_record
tools.description: Modify specific fields of an existing record without altering others.

base_id.description: The unique identifier of the Airtable base containing the table.

table_id.description: The table ID or table name that holds the record to update.

record_id.description: The unique identifier of the record to be updated.

fields.description: Map of field names or IDs to the new values; omitted fields remain unchanged.

id.description: The unique identifier of the updated record.

createdTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the record was originally created.

fields.description: Full set of field values reflecting the update.

scenarios.description: Single base with one table and two records — tests basic read/list operations
Two bases each with multiple tables and varied records — tests cross-base operations and field diversity
Rich workspace with three bases, multiple tables per base, diverse field types, and nested record data — tests filtering, lookup, and record manipulation
Empty workspace — no bases, tables, or records; tests graceful handling of empty state
